diagrams.title: UDP vs TCP Protocol Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side comparison showing the fundamental differences between UDP and TCP protocols, including their transport layer position, connection type, reliability guarantees, header size, speed, and typical use cases.
elements: Header Size Comparison (8 bytes vs 20 bytes)
diagrams.purpose: Explain the core differences between connectionless UDP and connection-oriented TCP protocols to establish foundation for flow control concepts

diagrams.title: TCP Sliding Window Flow Control Mechanism
diagrams.description: Visual representation of TCP's sliding window protocol showing how the sender and receiver coordinate data transfer through window size, sequence numbers, and acknowledgments (ACKs). Shows the window sliding as data is acknowledged.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate how TCP implements flow control using the sliding window mechanism to prevent sender from overwhelming the receiver

diagrams.title: TCP Three-Way Handshake and Connection States
diagrams.description: Step-by-step diagram showing the TCP connection establishment process (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK) between client and server, including the state transitions from LISTEN through ESTABLISHED, and the graceful connection termination (FIN, FIN-ACK, ACK).
elements: State Labels (LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RECEIVED, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT, CLOSE_WAIT, LAST_ACK)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how TCP establishes reliable connections through the three-way handshake and terminates connections properly, essential for understanding reliable data transfer
